text.title: Jailbreak Eval Suite Bootstrap Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring antipatterns in bootstrapped jailbreak suites with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Judge-not-calibrated — LLM-as-judge gates merges without κ measured against a hand-labelled holdout.
antipattern.symptom: Suite reports 100% pass while real attacks succeed; reverse case: spurious failures on benign refusals.
antipattern.root-cause: Judge prompts drift; without calibration we measure judge quality, not model behaviour.
antipattern.fix: Run judge-calibration-protocol on ≥30-case holdout; only ship judges with κ ≥ 0.7.

antipattern.description: Cherry-picked categories — suite covers only the easy-to-test families (refusal-bypass + DAN) and ignores cipher / multi-turn / code-context.
antipattern.symptom: One attack family covered, others silently regress.
antipattern.root-cause: Category list grows organically, never audited against a taxonomy.
antipattern.fix: Anchor to a published taxonomy (JailbreakBench, JBDistill); enforce ≥10 categories via validator.

antipattern.symptom: Low-volume category dropping from 100% to 50% does not move the global metric enough to trip the gate.
antipattern.fix: Per-category threshold (default 95%). Validator rejects global keys.

antipattern.description: No novelty rotation — the same 50 cases run forever; safety training memorises them.
antipattern.symptom: Pass-rate climbs to 100% over weeks despite model not actually improving.
antipattern.root-cause: Static suite + stochastic training = overfitting on the eval.
antipattern.fix: Rotate 10-20% of cases per quarter; keep an unpublished holdout.

antipattern.description: No CI gate — suite runs nightly or on-demand, not on PR merge.
antipattern.symptom: Bad prompt change lands; first sign is a real incident or a Monday dashboard glance.
antipattern.fix: Wire runner into PR check with trigger paths covering prompts/, tools/, model.cfg; regression >1pp blocks merge.
